assessment_items.question_content:
**Andy Warhol's *Gold Marilyn* references mass media in all of the following ways EXCEPT $\_\_\_\_\_\_$.**

![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/0b8a948018ab3a77fed3b6449deef2d0.jpeg)

Andy Warhol, Gold Marilyn Monroe, Silkscreen ink, Silkscreen ink on synthetic polymer
paint on canvas, 71.25 x 57 in. (211.4 x 144.7 cm), 1962 (MoMA)

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: Taken from a publicity still of Monroe, Warhol's silk screened photograph appears sloppy and mass produced.

choices.content: the garish colors of the figure

assessment_items.question_content:
**Although often considered a reference to popular culture, Warhol's *Gold Marilyn* might also be called $\_\_\_\_\_\_$ .**
![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/0b8a948018ab3a77fed3b6449deef2d0.jpeg)
Andy Warhol, *Gold Marilyn Monroe*, 1962, silkscreen ink on synthetic polymer
paint on canvas, 211.4 x 144.7 cm (MoMA)

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: Made soon after Monroe's suicide in 1962, Gold Marilyn might function as a memorial to the actress.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Roy Lichtenstein's *Rouen Cathedral Set V* references paintings by which earlier artist?**

![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/1afa195d1b326c16040322a1115b5dd2.jpeg)
Roy Lichtenstein, *Rouen Cathedral Set V*, 1969, oil and magna on canvas, 
3 canvases: 63-5/8 x 141-7/8 x 1-3/4 inches or 161.61 x 360.36 x 4.45 cm
(SFMOMA)

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: Roy Lichtenstein's *Rouen Cathedral Set V* references paintings by the French Impressionist painter, Monet.

assessment_items.question_content:
**What distinguishes Roy Lichtenstein's *Rouen Cathedral Set V* from Claude Monet's earlier image of Rouen Cathedral? **

![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/1afa195d1b326c16040322a1115b5dd2.jpeg)
Roy Lichtenstein, *Rouen Cathedral Set V*, 1969, oil and magna on canvas, 
3 canvases: 63-5/8 x 141-7/8 x 1-3/4 inches or 161.61 x 360.36 x 4.45 cm
(SFMOMA)

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: Although visually similar to the late serial paintings of Monet, Lichtenstein's use of hand-painted benday dots and repeated images allued to 20th century commercial techniques of mass production.

choices.content: Lichtenstein painted his canvas by hand.
Lichenstein created mulitple images of the same subject.
Lichenstein created a series of paintings.

assessment_items.question_content:
**What is one strategy that Oldenburg uses to elevate his popular subject matter?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: The monumental scale and representational style recall traditional sculpture, thus elevating his banal subject to the heroic ideals of the past.

choices.content: employing canvas as a sculptural material

assessment_items.question_content:
**Based on this discussion of Oldenburg's *Floor Cake*, which term does NOT describe Pop Art?**

![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/fc9b6bd2b11aa2a136a1f8f4ebc05478.jpeg)
Claes Oldenburg, *Floor Cake*, 1962, synthetic polymer paint and latex on canvas filled with foam rubber and cardboard boxes, 58.375 x 114.25 x 58.375 inches (MoMA)

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: Oldenberg's work is ironic and funny in its critique of cultural values associated with art.

assessment_items.question_content:
![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/42928676a6a096e78fa1896a5e0c8fff.jpeg)

**What distinguishes Warhol's Pop Art from traditional fine art practices?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: In contrast to the lofty subjects of traditional art, Warhol chose everyday objects of popular culture for his work.

choices.content: his use of everyday subject matter

assessment_items.question_content:
**Based on the speakers' remarks, what is a characteristic that distinguishes Andy Warhol's *Campbell's Soup Cans* as art? **

![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/92e0fb155e2495846e6fb322c2602fbf.jpeg)

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: By relocating an everyday object in an artistic context, Warhol forces us to look at it in a different way.

choices.content: the detailed rendering of the forms
the context in which it is understood
